nodes.title: Make the gallery a grid container
nodes.why: The cards need rows and columns, so CSS Grid gives direct control over the track structure and auto-placement.
nodes.tradeoff: Flexbox can wrap cards, but Grid makes the required column counts and row gaps easier to reason about.

nodes.title: Use the exact two-column mobile contract
nodes.why: The interview prompt requires two equal columns by default, and `minmax(0, 1fr)` prevents content minimums from widening a track.
nodes.alternative: Use `repeat(auto-fit, minmax(220px, 1fr))` for the main answer.
nodes.tradeoff: The fluid variant is useful in production, but the required answer should keep exact 2-to-4 column behavior.

nodes.title: Switch to four tracks at the desktop breakpoint
nodes.why: The breakpoint turns the same direct card children into four equal desktop columns without manual placement.
nodes.alternative: Hard-code four columns for all widths.
nodes.tradeoff: A desktop-only override preserves the interview contract while avoiding unreadably narrow mobile cards.

nodes.title: Let card content shrink inside its track
nodes.why: `min-width: 0` lets long titles wrap inside the grid cell instead of forcing horizontal overflow.
nodes.tradeoff: The explicit shrink rule is small, but it protects the gallery against real content edge cases.

nodes.title: Wrap long card headings
nodes.why: Long titles are a common card-grid failure mode, so headings should wrap instead of stretching the card.
nodes.alternative: Assume mock card titles are always short.
nodes.tradeoff: Aggressive wrapping can break long words, but it is safer than horizontal scroll in an interview prompt.

nodes.title: Keep the production variant as a grid
nodes.why: The production variant still benefits from Grid because the gallery is a two-dimensional card layout.
nodes.alternative: Switch to Flexbox because the column count is fluid.
nodes.tradeoff: Flexbox can flow cards, but Grid keeps track sizing and gaps more explicit.

nodes.title: Use auto-fit with a readable minimum width
nodes.why: `auto-fit` creates as many columns as fit while `220px` keeps each card readable.
nodes.alternative: Force the exact two-column mobile contract.
nodes.tradeoff: The production variant adapts better to containers, but it may show one column when two 220px tracks plus gap do not fit.

nodes.title: Accept the narrow one-column fallback
nodes.why: A one-column preview is expected when the container cannot fit two minimum-width tracks and the gap.
nodes.alternative: Lower the minimum width just to preserve two columns.
nodes.tradeoff: Lower minimums preserve more columns, but they can make cards less readable.

nodes.title: Keep overflow protections in the variant
nodes.why: The fluid layout still needs card-level shrink safety so long content does not widen the page.
nodes.alternative: Only set the gallery tracks and omit card rules.
nodes.tradeoff: Complete variant CSS is longer, but it keeps the loaded solution preview faithful to the main solution.

nodes.title: Wrap long headings in production too
nodes.why: Real product cards often have longer titles than mock data, so the fluid variant should keep the same long-text protection.
nodes.alternative: Let titles wrap only at normal word boundaries.
nodes.tradeoff: Normal wrapping looks cleaner for tidy content, but `anywhere` is more robust for edge cases.
